# Color Vision

**Source:** [Color & Contrast](https://colorandcontrast.com/) — A comprehensive guide to color for UI designers

_37 content fragments extracted from SPA bundle_

---

A color model defined by three primaries; red, green, and blue. The RGB model is based on principles of trichromatic vision and additive color mixing.

(Trichromatic vision) Color vision where all perceived hues are created by mixtures of three primary colors (e.g. red, green, & blue). Trichromacy is a photoreceptor-level understanding of color perception.

Color vision where all perceived hues are created by mixtures of three primary colors (e.g. red, green, & blue). Trichromacy is a

This simulation is intended to assist in demonstrating the concept behind the color opponent process. It is not scientifically accurate and does not account for color compositions or the luminosity channel (cones + rods).

Pages 140 of this book literally say color constancy does not exist.

An illusion created of depth or vibration created when certain colors are placed next to one another. Chromosteropsis is generally attributed to chromatic aberration.

Different wavelengths of light refract through a lens at different angles. This causes focal points to be at different depths of the retina resulting in an effect called chromostereopsis.

Different wavelengths of light refract through a lens at different angles. This causes focal points to be at different depths of the retina resulting in an effect called

Chromatic aberration can be caused by the lens and exaggerated by conditions such as astigmatism, myopia (nearsightedness), or hyperopia (farsightedness).

Defocused blue light from chromatic aberration can cause digital eye strain. This does not mean blue color should be avoided, as many colors (including white) include blue light.

Chromatic Aberration: Basic Optics, Chapter 6

This adaptation occurs by means of the different sensitivities of cones versus rods, a change in pupil size, cone photopigment concentration, and neural responsiveness.

in the eye has a different wavelength sensitivity than normal. Approximately 6% of males and 0.4% of femails are anomolous trichromats.

Most simulation tools imply a fixed experience for each vision type. Anomalous trichromats vary in the strength of their color deficiencies. Reliance on these tools (along with subjective decisions) can result in falsely identifying safe colors.

(Photophobia) A condition where bright lights hurt the eyes. Light sensitivity can be caused by migraines, corneal abrasion, conjuctivitis or other conditions.

Light sensitivity can be caused by migraines, corneal abrasion, conjuctivitis or other conditions such as

This illusion is result of improper color identification due to chromatic adaptation, or

The illusion is caused by chromatic adaptation. Opposing (

Coloration of an entire interface (such as blue-grays or browns) can result in chromatic adaptation. This can cause other colors in the interface to take on a different appearance.

A wide gamut implementation of the RGB color model used by modern retina displays.

Modern devices like smartphones, tablets, and retina screens use Display-P3. It provides a much wider collection of color, which are more colorful than sRGB.

An illusion where the appearance of a color is affected by previously viewed colors. Successive contrast is a result of the afterimage effect from chromatic adaptation.

is gaining browser support and used in retina displays.

The clarity or sharpness of vision, relative to optical blur. It is the measure of how well an image is in focus on the retina.

A color space representing the three different cone responses of trichromatic vision. LMS is used in studying and simulating color vision deficiencies.

An irregularity in the curvature in either the cornea or the inner lens of the eye. Astigmatism prevents light from properly focusing on the retina.

An irregularity in the curvature in either the cornea or the inner lens of the eye.

The pupil is the central opening in the iris muscle. The iris controls pupil size in order to regulate the amount of light entering the eye.

The retina is a thin layer at the backside of the eye comprised of photoreceptors and cells for signal processing.

The pigmented epithelium is the bottom layer of the retina, and is pigmented in order to absorb any remaining light that was not processed by the cones and rods.
